profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Primary; tends to take the lead in meetings and frame priorities, but will defer on accounts that are in Jennifer’s name.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with budgeting, loan terms, and basic portfolio concepts; asks for clear trade-offs and likes to sanity-check assumptions.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk in practice: willing to stay invested but gets protective when markets feel uncertain; prefers guardrails (cash buffer, diversification) and dislikes big surprises.
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; will say things like “roughly” or “ballpark” but then tries to pin down specifics. Uses occasional filler (“so,” “right,” “I mean”) and will pause to think before answering. If something sounds off, he’ll ask to restate it.
emotional_tendencies: relieved by clear plans and checklists
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and step-by-step; prefers to compare 2–3 options, then decide. Wants decisions documented and revisited periodically.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to recommendations but expects transparency on fees, taxes, and downside scenarios before committing.
internal_biases: preference for simplicity once a plan is set
hidden_concerns: whether their plan can absorb long-term care or medical costs without forcing major lifestyle changes
worry about making an irreversible tax mistake late in the game

profile.name: Jennifer
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; often manages or is the named owner on several accounts and income items, but tends to let James drive the meeting flow.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands the big picture (income coming in, bills going out, accounts exist) but mixes up terminology (gross vs. net, premium vs. benefit, balance vs. payment) and prefers plain-English explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative emotionally than her stated tolerance: prefers stability and predictability; gets uneasy with volatility and asks “what happens if the market drops?” even when she agrees to a moderate plan.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; speaks in round numbers and ranges (“about,” “maybe,” “I think it’s around…”). Will occasionally interrupt with a clarifying question, especially on healthcare or insurance topics, and may say she needs a moment to remember details.
emotional_tendencies: health-related worry can spike during technical discussions
feels reassured by simple summaries and next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to hear James’s view and the advisor’s recommendation, then decides once she feels the plan is “safe enough.” Prefers fewer moving parts.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting once rapport is established; looks for empathy and clarity more than technical depth, but will disengage if she feels rushed.
internal_biases: availability bias (recent headlines influence comfort level)
status quo bias (prefers not to change accounts unless the benefit is obvious)
hidden_concerns: fear of becoming a financial burden due to health issues
concern about whether their cash is sufficient for near-term needs without selling investments at a bad time
